# pypdf

pypdf is a free and open-source pure-python PDF library capable of splitting,
[merging](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user/merging-pdfs.html),
[cropping, and transforming](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user/cropping-and-transforming.html)
the pages of PDF files. It can also add
custom data, viewing options, and
[passwords](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user/encryption-decryption.html)
to PDF files. pypdf can
[retrieve text](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user/extract-text.html)
and
[metadata](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user/metadata.html)
from PDFs as well.

See [pdfly](https://github.com/py-pdf/pdfly) for a CLI application that uses pypdf to interact with PDFs.

## Installation

Install pypdf using pip:

```
pip install pypdf
```

For using pypdf with AES encryption or decryption, install extra dependencies:

```
pip install pypdf[crypto]
```

> **NOTE**: `pypdf` 3.1.0 and above include significant improvements compared to
> previous versions. Please refer to [the migration
> guide](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user/migration-1-to-2.html) for
> more information.

## Usage

```python
from pypdf import PdfReader

reader = PdfReader("example.pdf")
number_of_pages = len(reader.pages)
page = reader.pages[0]
text = page.extract_text()
```

pypdf can do a lot more, e.g. splitting, merging, reading and creating annotations, decrypting and encrypting. Check out the
[documentation](https://pypdf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) for additional usage
examples!

For questions and answers, visit
[StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/pypdf)
(tagged with [pypdf](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/pypdf)).

## Contributions

Maintaining pypdf is a collaborative effort. You can support the project by
writing documentation, helping to narrow down issues, and submitting code.
See the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) file for more information.

### Q&A

The experience pypdf users have covers the whole range from beginner to expert. You can contribute to the pypdf community by answering questions
on [StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/pypdf),
helping in [discussions](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/discussions),
and asking users who report issues for [MCVE](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example)'s (Code + example PDF!).


### Issues

arguments.expected_information: Definition of NameObject, renumber_table, and related helpers

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This module defines the core PDF object hierarchy used by the library, including NameObject and the renumbering/escaping logic, as well as IndirectObject and other primitive PDF types. It also provides encoding helpers (e.g., encode_pdfdocencoding) and a small type-check helper.

### Architecture
- Central abstract base: PdfObject, with common interfaces for cloning, hashing, and streaming.
- Concrete PDF primitives implemented as subclasses of PdfObject:
  - NullObject, BooleanObject
  - IndirectObject (represents indirect references and delegates to pointed objects)
  - FloatObject, NumberObject
  - ByteStringObject, TextStringObject
  - NameObject (specialized for PDF name objects and escaping)
- Utilities and helpers:
  - encode_pdfdocencoding: converts Unicode to PDFDocEncoding bytes
  - is_null_or_none: type guard for None or NullObject/indirect-null scenarios
- Name handling specifics:
  - NameObject defines renumber_table for escaping certain characters, and methods to produce/parse PDF name tokens.
- Reading/writing interfaces:
  - Many classes implement write_to_stream and read_from_stream (staticmethods) to serialize/deserialize PDF objects.

Interaction flow:
- IndirectObject handles references to objects across the PDF and can clone/relink objects into a destination writer.
- NameObject provides the encoding/escaping logic used when writing name tokens to a PDF stream (renumber) and parsing when reading (read_from_stream, unnumber).
- ByteStringObject and TextStringObject manage textual data with differing encodings, including decoding attempts and encoding strategies for writing.
- encode_pdfdocencoding is used by TextStringObject and NameObject to convert between Unicode and PDF’s encoding.

### Functions & Classes
- PdfObject — base class for all PDF objects. Importance: defines the common protocol (clone, hash, get_object, write_to_stream) used by all concrete PDF object types.
- PdfObject.hash_bin — abstract hash calculation placeholder. Importance: supports change-detection for object graphs.
- PdfObject.hash_value, hash_value_data — determine a stable hash for objects. Importance: object modification detection.
- PdfObject.replicate(pdf_dest) — clone into a writer without resolving links. Importance: supports incremental writing workflows.
- PdfObject.clone(pdf_dest, force_duplicate=False, ignore_fields=()) — abstract clone API. Importance: core cloning mechanism for objects across writers.
- PdfObject._reference_clone(clone, pdf_dest, force_duplicate=False) — internal helper to reference/copy objects when cloning, handling indirect references and object tables. Importance: central cloning logic for linked objects.
- PdfObject.get_object() — resolve to the underlying object. Importance: used when a PdfObject might be a proxy (e.g., IndirectObject).
- PdfObject.write_to_stream(stream, encryption_key=None) — abstract serialization. Importance: core I/O contract for all objects.
- NullObject — represents a null PDF object.
  - NullObject.clone(pdf_dest, force_duplicate=False, ignore_fields=()) — clones via reference mechanism. Importance: standard behavior for nulls in object graphs.
  - NullObject.hash_bin — hash for null object.
  - NullObject.write_to_stream — writes b"null". Importance: standard PDF null representation.
  - NullObject.read_from_stream — parses "null" token from a stream. Importance: deserialization.
  - NullObject.__repr__, __eq__ — debugging/identity utilities.
- BooleanObject(value) — boolean primitive.
  - BooleanObject.clone(pdf_dest, ...) — clone with reference mechanism. Importance: preserve identity semantics across writers.
  - BooleanObject.hash_bin — hash by class and value.
  - BooleanObject.__eq__, __repr__ — equality and textual representation ("True"/"False").
  - BooleanObject.write_to_stream — writes b"true"/b"false". Importance: serialization.
  - BooleanObject.read_from_stream — parses "true" or "false" from a stream. Importance: deserialization.
- IndirectObject(idnum, generation, pdf) — represents an indirect reference in a PDF.
  - IndirectObject.__hash__ — hash by idnum, generation, and pdf identity.
  - IndirectObject.hash_bin — include idnum, generation, pdf identity.
  - IndirectObject.replicate(pdf_dest) — clone as a new indirect reference.
  - IndirectObject.clone(pdf_dest, force_duplicate=False, ignore_fields=()) — clone into writer, potentially reusing or duplicating the object and updating reference tables.
  - IndirectObject.indirect_reference (property) — returns self; used for compatibility/typing with other objects.
  - IndirectObject.get_object() — resolve to the pointed object via the underlying reader/writer.
  - IndirectObject.__deepcopy__ — shallow copy semantics for deepcopy operations.
  - IndirectObject._get_object_with_check — fetch pointed object with safety check.
  - IndirectObject.__getattr__, __getitem__, __contains__, __iter__, __float__, __int__, __str__ — delegate to the pointed object; enable IndirectObject to behave as if it were the underlying object.
  - IndirectObject.__repr__, __eq__, __ne__ — identity/equality semantics.
  - IndirectObject.write_to_stream — writes the indirect reference syntax "idnum generation R".
  - IndirectObject.read_from_stream(stream, pdf) — parses an indirect object reference (idnum, generation, R) from a stream.
- FloatObject(float, PdfObject) — floating-point number.
  - FloatObject.__new__(cls, value="0.0", context=None) — constructs a FloatObject; fallback to 0.0 on invalid input with a warning.
  - FloatObject.clone(pdf_dest, ...) — clone with reference mechanism.
  - FloatObject.hash_bin — includes numeric value for change detection.
  - FloatObject.mrepr (named as myrepr) — pretty-print with controlled precision.
  - FloatObject.__repr__ — string representation via myrepr.
  - FloatObject.as_numeric — returns numeric float value.
  - FloatObject.write_to_stream — writes textual numeric representation.
- NumberObject(int, PdfObject) — integer number.
  - NumberObject.NumberPattern — regex to detect non-numeric characters.
  - NumberObject.__new__(cls, value) — constructs an int-based PDF object; falls back to 0 with a warning if invalid.
  - NumberObject.clone(pdf_dest, ...) — clone via reference.
  - NumberObject.hash_bin — hash by class and numeric value.
  - NumberObject.as_numeric — numeric value representation.
  - NumberObject.write_to_stream — writes integer as text.
  - NumberObject.read_from_stream(stream) — reads a number; may return a FloatObject if a dot is found (i.e., numeric int or float).
- ByteStringObject(bytes, PdfObject) — raw byte string with unknown encoding.
  - ByteStringObject.clone(pdf_dest, ...) — clone via reference.
  - ByteStringObject.hash_bin — hash for the raw bytes.
  - ByteStringObject.original_bytes (property) — returns the original bytes for compatibility.
  - ByteStringObject.write_to_stream — writes as a PDF hex-like string in angle brackets, hex-encoded.
  - ByteStringObject.__str__ — attempts decoding using a set of charsets to produce text; raises PdfReadError if decoding fails.
- TextStringObject(str, PdfObject) — decoded text string object.
  - TextStringObject.__new__(cls, value) — constructs a TextStringObject, handling potential UTF-16 BOMs or PDFDocEncoding autodetection; sets autodetect flags and stores original bytes.
  - TextStringObject.clone(pdf_dest, ...) — clone with deep copy of metadata (original bytes, autodetect flags).
  - TextStringObject.hash_bin — hash based on class and original bytes.
  - TextStringObject.original_bytes (property) — returns the original bytes if available; otherwise falls back to get_original_bytes().
  - TextStringObject.get_original_bytes() — reconstructs original bytes depending on autodetection method (UTF-16 with BOM or PDFDocEncoding).
  - TextStringObject.get_encoded_bytes() — returns a PDF-compatible encoded byte sequence, preferring original bytes, otherwise encoding attempts, with fallback to UTF-16BE/UTF-16LE as needed.
  - TextStringObject.write_to_stream — writes the string as a PDF literal string, escaping non-alphanumeric characters.
- NameObject(str, PdfObject) — represents a PDF name object with escaping semantics.
  - delimiter_pattern (class var) — regex for delimiters that terminate a name token.
  - surfix (class var) — always b"/" as the first byte for a name in PDF syntax.
  - renumber_table (class var) — mapping used to escape special characters by replacing with #HH hex escapes (plus a few explicit mappings).
  - CHARSETS (class var) — list of character encodings to try when reading a name (e.g., "utf-8", "gbk", "latin1").
  - clone(pdf_dest, ...) — clone via reference for NameObject.
  - hash_bin — hash by class and the NameObject value.
  - write_to_stream — serializes the name using renumber().
  - renumber() — converts the NameObject into its PDF-encoded form, escaping non-safe characters using renumber_table or hex escapes; emits deprecation warnings if the first character is not '/'.
  - unnumber(sin: bytes) — static helper to revert the #HH escapes back to normal bytes.
  - read_from_stream(stream, pdf) — parses a NameObject from a stream, ensuring the name begins with '/', unescaping with unnumber, and decoding using CHARSETS; handles errors and falls back with a warning if pdf.strict is disabled.
- encode_pdfdocencoding(unicode_string: str) -> bytes
  - Encodes a Python string into PDFDocEncoding bytes, raising UnicodeEncodeError if a character cannot be translated. Importance: used for efficient, human-readable string encoding in PDFs.
- is_null_or_none(x: Any) -> TypeGuard[Union[None, NullObject, IndirectObject]]

Provides core PDF object models used by the library, with a focus on how NameObject (and related primitives) are encoded/decoded, serialized, and reconstructed. It defines a hierarchy of PdfObject-derived classes (NullObject, BooleanObject, IndirectObject, FloatObject, NumberObject, ByteStringObject, TextStringObject, NameObject) and helper utilities for encoding, decoding, and object management.

### Architecture
- Core base: PdfObject acts as the abstract interface for all concrete PDF objects, declaring cloning, hashing, and streaming behavior.
- Concrete primitives: NullObject, BooleanObject, IndirectObject, FloatObject, NumberObject, ByteStringObject, TextStringObject, and NameObject implement the PdfObject interface for their respective PDF data types.
- Indirection and references: IndirectObject models an indirect reference to an object within a PDF, including logic for cloning across writers and resolving the pointed object.
- Encoding/decoding utilities: encode_pdfdocencoding handles PDFDocEncoding, and is_null_or_none provides a type-guard helper.
- Name handling: NameObject is the key class of interest for precise modifications. It includes mechanisms to encode/decode PDF name objects, including handling of special characters via renumber/unnumber, and parsing from streams using a delimiter-aware reader.
- Global constants: delimiter_pattern (regex for valid name delimitation), surfix (the leading slash), renumber_table (mapping for escaping characters), and CHARSETS (fallback decodings).

### Functions & Classes
- PdfObject — base protocol/class for all PDF objects; defines hashing, cloning, and streaming hooks. Importance: foundational interface for the object hierarchy.
- PdfObject.hash_func — class attribute specifying the hash function used by hash-related operations. Importance: used by hash_bin/hash_value logic in subclasses.
- PdfObject.indirect_reference — optional attribute used to link to an IndirectObject in cloning workflows. Importance: enables correct cross-reference handling.

- NullObject — represents the PDF null object.
  - NullObject.clone — returns a cloned null object via _reference_clone.
  - NullObject.hash_bin — provides a stable hash for null values.
  - NullObject.write_to_stream — writes b"null" to the stream.
  - NullObject.read_from_stream — reads 4 bytes and returns a NullObject; validates literal "null".
  - NullObject.__repr__ — returns "NullObject".
  - NullObject.__eq__ — equality check (type-match).

- BooleanObject — represents a boolean (true/false).
  - BooleanObject.__init__ — stores value.
  - BooleanObject.clone — clones via _reference_clone.
  - BooleanObject.hash_bin — includes the boolean value in hash.
  - BooleanObject.__eq__ — supports comparison with another BooleanObject or a native bool.
  - BooleanObject.__repr__ — returns "True" or "False" representation.
  - BooleanObject.write_to_stream — writes "true" or "false".
  - BooleanObject.read_from_stream — reads "true" or "false" (with a short handling for "fals" + extra char).
  - Importance: fundamental scalar type.

- IndirectObject — models an indirect reference (object id, generation, and the PDF it belongs to).
  - IndirectObject.__init__ — stores idnum, generation, pdf.
  - IndirectObject.__hash__ — hash based on idnum, generation, and pdf identity.
  - IndirectObject.hash_bin — hash of (type, idnum, generation, pdf identity).
  - IndirectObject.clone — clones or reuses via writer’s object table; handles force_duplicate.
  - IndirectObject.replicate — creates a new indirect reference for a destination PDF writer.
  - IndirectObject.indirect_reference (property) — returns self; used for compatibility with PdfObject interface.
  - IndirectObject.get_object — resolves to the pointed object via pdf.get_object(self).
  - IndirectObject.__deepcopy__ — shallow copy semantics for identity preservation.
  - IndirectObject._get_object_with_check — resolves and ensures not to point to another IndirectObject.
  - IndirectObject.__getattr__ / __getitem__ / __contains__ / __iter__ / __float__ / __int__ / __str__ — delegate to the pointed object, enabling natural usage of indirects.
  - IndirectObject.__repr__ — string representation showing the reference.
  - IndirectObject.__eq__ / __ne__ — logical equality by idnum, generation, and pdf.
  - IndirectObject.write_to_stream — writes "idnum generation R".
  - IndirectObject.read_from_stream — parses an indirect object reference (id, generation, "R") from a stream.
  - Importance: central to PDF object graph and cross-reference handling; essential for renumbering and cloning workflows.

- FLOAT_WRITE_PRECISION — constant (8). Importance: determines numeric formatting precision for floats.

- FloatObject — floating-point numeric value (extends float).
  - FloatObject.__new__ — constructs a FloatObject, with fallback to 0.0 on invalid input and a warning.
  - FloatObject.clone — clones via _reference_clone.
  - FloatObject.hash_bin — hashes based on the numeric value.
  - FloatObject.mrepr — internal helper to format float with controlled precision (myrepr).
  - FloatObject.__repr__ — uses myrepr.
  - FloatObject.as_numeric — returns the numeric value.
  - FloatObject.write_to_stream — writes the textual representation to the stream.
  - Importance: supports real-number representation in PDFs.

- NumberObject — integer numeric value.
  - NumberObject.NumberPattern — regex used to detect non-digit characters.
  - NumberObject.__new__ — constructs an int-based NumberObject, fallback to 0 with warning.
  - NumberObject.clone — clones via _reference_clone.
  - NumberObject.hash_bin — hash includes numeric value.
  - NumberObject.as_numeric — returns int value as numeric.
  - NumberObject.write_to_stream — writes ASCII decimal representation.
  - NumberObject.read_from_stream — reads a number, returns NumberObject or FloatObject depending on presence of a decimal point.
  - Importance: primary integer type in PDFs; interacts with streams and parsing.

- ByteStringObject — represents a byte string with unknown encoding.
  - ByteStringObject.clone — clones via _reference_clone.
  - ByteStringObject.hash_bin — hash based on bytes.
  - ByteStringObject.original_bytes (property) — for compatibility with TextStringObject.
  - ByteStringObject.write_to_stream — writes as a hex-encoded PDF string (e.g., <...>).
  - ByteStringObject.__str__ — attempts to decode using several encodings; raises PdfReadError if decoding fails.
  - Importance: handles non-textual data stored as strings in PDFs.

- TextStringObject — text string decoded to Python str.
  - TextStringObject.__new__ — handles initialization and autodetection for PDFDocEncoding vs UTF-16; may adjust encoding strategy based on BOMs.
  - TextStringObject.clone — clones while preserving autodetection state.
  - TextStringObject.hash_bin — hashed on original bytes.
  - TextStringObject.original_bytes (property) — exposes original bytes if available.
  - TextStringObject.get_original_bytes — reconstructs original bytes if autodetected.
  - TextStringObject.get_encoded_bytes — yields PDF-encoded bytes (PDFDocEncoding, or UTF-16 with BOM if necessary).
  - TextStringObject.write_to_stream — writes encoded bytes as a PDF string with parentheses, escaping non-alphanumeric characters.
  - Importance: handles textual data with robust encoding/decoding logic for PDFs.

- NameObject — represents a PDF name object (leading slash, encoded as needed).
  - delimiter_pattern — compiled regex rb"\s+|[\(\)<>\[\]{}/%]": used to determine where a name ends when reading from a stream.
  - surfix — b"/": leading slash indicator for a name.
  - renumber_table — classvar mapping for escaping special characters to their #HH hex representations (e.g., "#": b"#23", "(": b"#28", etc.), plus a general mapping for characters with ord < 33.
  - clone — standard _reference_clone-based cloning.
  - hash_bin — includes the NameObject itself in the hash.
  - write_to_stream — writes the encoded name via renumber().
  - renumber — encodes the name to PDF syntax:
    - Verifies the first character is "/" (emits deprecation warning if not).
    - For each subsequent character, escapes characters > "~" as #HH hex, otherwise uses renumber_table for common escapes or the raw character.
  - unnumber — static method to decode #HH escapes back into bytes:
    - Scans for "#" sequences and hex-decodes the two following hex digits.
  - CHARSETS — ("utf-8", "gbk", "latin1"): used to decode names when reading from a stream.
  - read_from_stream — static method to read a NameObject from a stream:
    - Reads the initial "/" (surfix) and then reads until the delimiter_pattern.
    - Applies unnumber to decode escapes, then tries various encodings in CHARSETS to produce a Python string.
    - On failure and if pdf.strict is False, falls back to charmapped decoding and issues a warning.
    - If strict, raises an error.
  - Importance: central to handling PDF Name objects; the exact code for renumber, unnumber, and read_from_stream is critical for precise modification planning.

- encode_pdfdocencoding(unicode_string) — helper to encode a Python string into PDFDocEncoding bytes via a reverse map.
  - Raises UnicodeEncodeError if a character cannot be represented in PDFDocEncoding.
  - Importance: used by TextStringObject/NameObject handling to encode/decode strings for PDF compatibility.

- is_null_or_none(x) — helper type-guard to determine if x is None or a NullObject or an IndirectObject pointing to NullObject.
  - TypeGuard[Union[None, NullObject, IndirectObject]]: informs static typing about null-like objects.
  - Importance: utility to safely handle null-like values in higher-level code.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file contains the exact implementation of NameObject, including:
  - delimiter_pattern (regex controlling token boundaries for names)
  - renumber_table (escaping rules for special characters in names)
  - renumber (produces the proper PDF Name encoding)
  - unnumber (decodes #HH escapes)
  - read_from_stream (parses a NameObject from a stream with proper encoding fallbacks)
  - surfix (leading slash indicator)
  - CHARSETS (decoding attempts for read_from_stream)
- Because the agent is planning precise modifications to NameObject behavior (especially how names are serialized and parsed), this file provides the authoritative source. Any changes to NameObject’s encoding/decoding, escape handling, or stream parsing should be staged here with attention to the exact logic and edge cases present in renumber, unnumber, and read_from_stream.

Notes:
- Many other classes in this file establish the broader object model that NameObject interacts with (e.g., cloning behavior via _reference_clone, indirect object handling, and stream I/O). While the agent’s focus is NameObject, understanding these interactions is important for ensuring compatibility when modifying NameObject within the object graph.

### File Overview
This module defines the core PDF object model used by the library, including a base PdfObject class, concrete PDF object types (NullObject, BooleanObject, IndirectObject, FloatObject, NumberObject, ByteStringObject, TextStringObject, NameObject), and related utilities for encoding/decoding, cloning, and I/O. It specifically includes the NameObject class and its methods (renumber, unnumber) and supporting data (renumber_table, CHARSETS), which are central to how PDF name objects are serialized and parsed.

### Architecture
- Core abstraction: PdfObject serves as the base interface for all PDF object types, providing common cloning, hashing, and streaming hooks.
- Concrete object types: Each PDF type (NullObject, BooleanObject, IndirectObject, FloatObject, NumberObject, ByteStringObject, TextStringObject, NameObject) implements type-specific behavior for cloning, hashing, and serialization.
- Indirect object handling: IndirectObject wraps a reference to an object within a PDF document and provides mechanisms to clone and resolve the pointed object, supporting incremental and full-document operations.
- Name handling: NameObject handles PDF name objects, including escaping and unescaping via renumber and unnumber, respectively. It uses a renumber_table to map disallowed characters to their escaped forms and provides parsing/reading support via read_from_stream.
- Encoding utilities: Functions like encode_pdfdocencoding and is_null_or_none support string encoding and type checks, used across multiple object types.
- Utilities and constants: Several helper utilities from the project are used (e.g., _pdfdoc_encoding_rev, deprecate_no_replacement, logger_warning), plus a FLOAT_WRITE_PRECISION constant governing float-to-string formatting.

### Functions & Classes
- PdfObject — base class for all PDF objects.
  - hash_bin(self) -> int — abstract-like method to be implemented by subclasses for change-detection hashing.
  - hash_value_data(self) -> bytes — returns a byte representation used in hash_value.
  - hash_value(self) -> bytes — combines class name hash with hash_value_data for change tracking.
  - replicate(self, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol) -> "PdfObject" — clones object into a target writer without linking (used in incremental cloning).
  - clone(self, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol, force_duplicate: bool = False, ignore_fields: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, int]]] = ()) -> "PdfObject" — clone/duplicate logic for the object; to be implemented by subclasses.
  - _reference_clone(self, clone: Any, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol, force_duplicate: bool = False) -> PdfObjectProtocol — internal helper to clone while maintaining/creating indirect references, handling object identity and incremental updates.
  - get_object(self) -> Optional["PdfObject"] — resolve indirect references if needed.
  - write_to_stream(self, stream: StreamType, encryption_key: Union[None, str, bytes] = None) -> None — serialize object to a stream.
- NullObject(PdfObject)
  - clone(self, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol, force_duplicate: bool = False, ignore_fields: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, int]]] = ()) -> "NullObject" — clone into pdf_dest via reference-based cloning.
  - hash_bin(self) -> int — hash for change-detection (based on class only).
  - write_to_stream(self, stream: StreamType, encryption_key: Union[None, str, bytes] = None) -> None — emit "null".
  - read_from_stream(stream: StreamType) -> "NullObject" — read and validate a null object from stream.
  - __repr__(self) -> str — string representation.
  - __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool — equality check.
- BooleanObject(PdfObject)
  - __init__(self, value: Any) -> None
  - clone(self, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol, force_duplicate: bool = False, ignore_fields: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, int]]] = ()) -> "BooleanObject" — clone into pdf_dest.
  - hash_bin(self) -> int — hash based on class and boolean value.
  - __eq__(self, o: object, /) -> bool — equality with another BooleanObject or Python bool.
  - __repr__(self) -> str — "True" or "False".
  - write_to_stream(self, stream: StreamType, encryption_key: Union[None, str, bytes] = None) -> None — emit "true" or "false".
  - read_from_stream(stream: StreamType) -> "BooleanObject" — read boolean value from stream.
- IndirectObject(PdfObject)
  - __init__(self, idnum: int, generation: int, pdf: Any) -> None
  - __hash__(self) -> int
  - hash_bin(self) -> int
  - replicate(self, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol) -> "PdfObject" — create a reference to be written into a different document.
  - clone(self, pdf_dest: PdfWriterProtocol, force_duplicate: bool = False, ignore_fields: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, int]]] = ()) -> "IndirectObject" — clone/duplicate behavior for indirect references.
  - indirect_reference (property) -> "IndirectObject" — identity wrapper for the reference.
  - get_object(self) -> Optional["PdfObject"] — resolve to the actual pointed object.
  - __deepcopy__(self, memo: Any) -> "IndirectObject"
  - _get_object_with_check(self) -> Optional["PdfObject"] — internal retrieval with safety check to avoid circular references.
  - __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any — delegate attribute access to the pointed object.
  - __getitem__(self, key: Any) -> Any — delegate item access to the pointed object.
  - __contains__(self, key: Any) -> bool — membership test against the pointed object.
  - __iter__(self) -> Any — iterate over the pointed object.
  - __float__(self) -> str — convert by evaluating the pointed object as float.
  - __int__(self) -> int — convert by evaluating the pointed object as int.
  - __str__(self) -> str — string representation of the pointed object.
  - __repr__(self) -> str
  - __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool
  - __ne__(self, other: object) -> bool
  - write_to_stream(self, stream: StreamType, encryption_key: Union[None, str, bytes] = None) -> None — write an indirect reference as "idnum generation R".
  - read_from_stream(stream: StreamType, pdf: Any) -> "IndirectObject" — parse an indirect reference from stream.
- FLOAT_WRITE_PRECISION = 8
  - Global constant controlling floating-point string precision used when writing FloatObject values.
- FloatObject(float, PdfObject)
  - __new__(cls, value: Any = "0.0", context: Optional[Any] = None) -> "FloatObject" — construct and coerce to float; on error, fall back to 0.0 with a warning.
  - clone(self, pdf_dest: Any, force_duplicate: bool = False, ignore_fields: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, int]]] = ()) -> "FloatObject"
  - hash_bin(self) -> int
  - myrepr(self) -> str — formatted string with controlled precision.
  - __repr__(self) -> str
  - as_numeric(self) -> float
  - write_to_stream(self, stream: StreamType, encryption_key: Union[None, str, bytes] = None) -> None
- NumberObject(int, PdfObject)
  - NumberPattern = re.compile(b"[^+-.0-9]")
  - __new__(cls, value: Any) -> "NumberObject"
  - clone(self, pdf_dest: Any, force_duplicate: bool = False, ignore_fields: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, int]]] = ()) -> "NumberObject"
  - hash_bin(self) -> int
  - as_numeric(self) -> int
  - write_to_stream(self, stream: StreamType, encryption_key: Union[None, str, bytes] = None) -> None
  - read_from_stream(stream: StreamType) -> Union["NumberObject", "FloatObject"]
- ByteStringObject(bytes, PdfObject)

Per the PDF specification, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters. The current escaping mapping (NameObject.renumber_table) handles several special characters, but it does not include angle brackets, square brackets, or curly braces, which are also delimiters. You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",

  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",

}
```

```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",

  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```

## PR Body
Per the PDF specification, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters. The current escaping mapping (NameObject.renumber_table) handles several special characters, but it does not include angle brackets, square brackets, or curly braces, which are also delimiters. As a result, names containing these characters could be serialized without proper escaping, potentially producing invalid PDFs when appending/merging content.

Proposed change:
- Extend NameObject.renumber_table to escape the characters: <, >, [, ], {, }

  - This aligns with the existing escaping approach for other characters (e.g., '#', '(', ')', '/', '%') and ensures any name containing these delimiters is safely serialized.

Impact:
- Serialization of Name objects will consistently escape these additional delimiters, preventing creation of invalid PDFs in edge cases where names contain such characters.
- No public API changes; behavior is more broadly correct and spec-compliant.

Notes:
- A minimal patch should only add the six key-value pairs to NameObject.renumber_table in pypdf/generic/_base.py.
- Consider adding/adjusting unit tests to cover renumbering of names containing these delimiters (e.g., "/Name<", "/Name[abc]", etc.).
